Original Title: You Are the Medicine: 13 Moons of Indigenous Wisdom, Ancestral Connection and Animal Spirit Guidance
Author: Frost, Asha
Source Background for Indigenous Author: Literal is employed to ensure alignment with the author's self-identification and preserve their indigeneity per the biographical record. This approach is utilized over external identifiers (e.g., Wikidata URIs) because available URIs do not accurately reflect the specific identity documented in the source. Holds membership at Neyaashiinigmiig (Cape Croker) First Nation speakerscanada.com/keynote-speaker/asha-frost. Per source, "Cape Croker Indian Reserve... was officially named “Neyaashiinigmiing” on January 21, 1992. It means “point of land surrounded on three side by water.” nawash.ca/about-chippewas-of-nawash. Per source "Chippewas of Nawash Unceded First Nation (Ojibwe: Neyaashiinigmiing Anishinaabek) is an Anishinaabek First Nation from the Bruce Peninsula region in Ontario, Canada. Along with the Saugeen First Nation, they form the Saugeen Ojibway Nation. The Chippewas of Nawash Unceded First Nation currently has a registered membership of 2758 individuals, as of December, 2020. Approximately 700 members live on the main reserve, Neyaashiinigmiing 27 (formerly known as Cape Croker). The First Nation has 3 reserves, Neyaashiinigmiing 27, Cape Croker Hunting Ground 60B and Saugeen and Cape Croker Fishing Islands 1. The size of all reserves is 8083.70 hectares (31.21 sq. mi.)" whose.land/en/communities/chippewas-of-nawash-unceded-first-nation.
Subject Heading: Health; Worldview
